section_info.title: Introduction: Protecting Cardholder Data with PCI Security Standards

framework_info.framework_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
framework_info.framework_description: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is a set of security standards designed to ensure that all companies that accept, process, store or transmit credit card information maintain a secure environment. PCI DSS applies to all entities involved in payment card processing including merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.

policies.policy_title: Protecting Cardholder Data with PCI Security Standards
policies.policy_description: The twentieth century U.S. criminal Willie Sutton was said to rob banks because “that’s where the money is.” The same motivation in our digital age makes merchants the new target for financial fraud.
policies.scope: Payment card transactions and processing systems
policies.objective: To thwart theft of cardholder data
policies.policy_type: Framework: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

framework_info.framework_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
framework_info.framework_description: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is a set of security standards designed to ensure that all companies that accept, process, store or transmit credit card information maintain a secure environment. PCI DSS applies to all entities involved in payment card processing including merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.

policies.policy_description: This Guide provides supplemental information that does not replace or supersede PCI SSC Security Standards or their supporting documents.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data
policies.objective: To help protect your payment card transaction environment and how to apply it
policies.policy_type: Framework: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

policies.policy_description: Identifying all locations of cardholder data, taking an inventory of your IT assets and business processes for payment card processing and analyzing them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish clear requirements and guidelines for assess, ensuring organizational compliance and operational effectiveness. Specific objectives include: (1) establishing clear expectations and requirements for organizational behavior; (2) ensuring consistent implementation of best practices across all applicable areas; (3) minimizing operational risks and compliance gaps; (4) promoting accountability and responsibility among all stakeholders; (5) enabling effective monitoring and continuous improvement; and (6) supporting overall organizational objectives and strategic goals.
policies.policy_type: Step in adhering to the PCI DSS

policies.policy_description: Fixing identified vulnerabilities, securely removing any unnecessary cardholder data storage, and implementing secure business processes.
policies.scope: All entities that store, process or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish clear requirements and guidelines for repair, ensuring organizational compliance and operational effectiveness. policies.policy_type: Step in adhering to the PCI DSS

policies.policy_description: Documenting assessment and remediation details, and submitting compliance reports to the acquiring bank and card brands you do business with (or other requesting entity if you’re a service provider).

section_info.title: Implementing PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes

framework_info.framework_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
framework_info.framework_description: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is a set of security standards designed to ensure that all companies that accept, process, store or transmit credit card information maintain a secure environment. PCI DSS applies to all entities involved in payment card processing including merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.

policies.policy_title: Implementing PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes
policies.policy_description: This Guide provides supplemental information that does not replace or supersede PCI SSC Security Standards or their supporting documents.
policies.scope: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) globally applies to all entities that store, process or transmit cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
policies.objective: To help protect your payment card transaction environment and how to apply it.
policies.policy_type: Framework: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

policies.scope: Identifying all locations of cardholder data, taking an inventory of your IT assets and business processes for payment card processing and analyzing them for vulnerabilities that could expose cardholder data.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish clear requirements and guidelines for assess, ensuring organizational compliance and operational effectiveness. Specific objectives include: (1) establishing clear expectations and requirements for organizational behavior; (2) ensuring consistent implementation of best practices across all applicable areas; (3) minimizing operational risks and compliance gaps; (4) promoting accountability and responsibility among all stakeholders; (5) enabling effective monitoring and continuous improvement; and (6) supporting overall organizational objectives and strategic goals.

policies.scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, and processes involved in assess. The scope encompasses all information systems, networks, applications, databases, and associated infrastructure components. It includes all employees, contractors, vendors, and third-party service providers who have access to organizational systems or data. The policy covers both on-premises and cloud-based environments, including mobile devices and remote access scenarios.
